title: Perfusion/ Fixation for cryostat slicing for HHC
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dobf2","text":"This protocol details the Perfusion and Fixation for cryostat slicing for HHC.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Anesthetize the animal with sodium pentobarbital ( 100 mg/kg et 7 mg/ml ) by i.p. injection with a 27G needle.
Confirm the absence of pain reflex (paw pinch).
Perfuse the animal with 50 mL of PBS followed by 50 mL of PFA 4% by trans-cardiac injection with a butterfly needle while the right atrium is detached from the superior vena cava.
Extract the brain and immerse it 48:00:00 in PFA 4% followed by 48:00:00 - 96:00:00 in sucrose 30% (until the initially floating brain drops to the bottom of the tube) Composition:
Using dry ice, cool isopentane to -30 °C and submerge the brains for 00:00:15 - 00:00:30 in the freezing solution before storing them at -80 °C .
creator: Nicolas Giguère
